what: sensor-fixed hot-pixel defects on BLUE Bayer sites — the '2 blue stationary dots/streaks' the user sees in every raw frame (user-reported from set-02 DSC_7569/7573 viewing; NX Studio, portrait mount)
method: cross-flat coincidence: Siril find_hot 6 6 on all four on-rig per-set sky flats (independent un-registered medians of sets 01/03/04/05) + the master dark; a sensor-fixed defect is constant across a set's lights, so even the winsorized flat build keeps it and it must appear at the same sensor (x,y) in every flat. Channel ID: parity medians on the flat (G twins measured on the (0,0)/(1,1) diagonal) + BAYERPAT='RGGB' header — Nikon RGGB in FITS bottom-up row order = GBRG in the array frame, so the defect parity (1,0) is the B site; confirmed by direct hot-pixel localization in the array (values 267-558 ADU on a 66-126 ADU dark-subtracted sky)

defects_findhot_coords.name: dot A
defects_findhot_coords.note: PAIR of adjacent B-sites 2 px apart, same row — demosaics into one blue blob >1 px; near the left frame edge (sensor frame)

defects_findhot_coords.name: dot B
defects_findhot_coords.note: second same-row B-site pair — the other visible blue blob

defects_findhot_coords.name: single 1
defects_findhot_coords.note: single B-site hot pixel, weaker visual footprint

defects_findhot_coords.name: single 2
defects_findhot_coords.note: single B-site hot pixel, weaker visual footprint

presence: sets 01/03/04/05 instrument-confirmed (same positions in all four flats AND the master dark at 6-sigma); set-02 by the user's eyes + continuity (a sensor defect does not skip one 35-min window). Being sensor-fixed, they are in every frame of every set and will appear in other nights' data from this body until serviced.
chain_disposition: ALREADY NEUTRALIZED in the production chain: all positions are in the master dark's 3-sigma bad-pixel map (find_hot 3 3, 33,998 entries), and light calibration mandatorily applies -cc=dark 3 3 (single-sourced, scripts/stack/calibrate_light.sh) which replaces exactly these pixels per frame — they reach no stack as dots. They also sit IN the sky flats (a constant is not an outlier to the winsorized build) — harmless: cc replaces those same pixels in the lights after the division. The faint drift-aligned streaks in renders are the SUB-3-sigma warm population (BACKLOG item 11, quantified 0.34-0.48 ADU per half), not these mapped-and-killed pixels.
in_bounds_note: flats/darks are calibration masters, not the deliverable; find_hot/stat did the detection, the array read localized tool-found positions for channel classification — instrument work, report-only
watch: re-run the cross-flat coincidence when a new session's flats build (new hot pixels appear over sensor life; the dark refresh + bad-pixel map re-derive per session)
